completion_promises.criterion_text: All six entity models defined with Pydantic v2 BaseModel
completion_promises.evidence: Defined GateDecision, CapabilityResolution, OverrideEvent, CalibrationAdjustment, SessionOutcome, and CalibrationEvent in src/forge/memory/models.py. Each subclasses pydantic.BaseModel and uses the v2 ConfigDict-based model_config (no v1 inner Config class). Verified by tests/unit/test_models.py::TestPydanticV2BaseModel which asserts BaseModel inheritance and the v2 surface (model_config, model_fields) for all six models, plus that all six are exported from the module __all__.

completion_promises.criterion_text: entity_id field documented as 'MUST be sourced from SQLite UUID, never generated at write time' (ASSUM-007 resolution)
completion_promises.evidence: Every UUID-typed entity_id field on the five pipeline-history models (GateDecision, CapabilityResolution, OverrideEvent, CalibrationAdjustment, SessionOutcome) carries the description 'MUST be sourced from SQLite UUID, never generated at write time (ASSUM-007 resolution).' The module docstring also states the rule. Verified by TestEntityIdSourcedFromSqliteDocstring, which case-insensitively asserts both 'sqlite' and 'never generated at write time' appear in each field's description, plus a separate parametrised test that the annotation is exactly UUID for those five models.

completion_promises.criterion_text: gate_decision_ids on SessionOutcome documented as ordered ascending by decided_at (ASSUM-008 resolution)
completion_promises.evidence: SessionOutcome.gate_decision_ids has a Field(description=...) that explicitly states 'The list MUST be sorted ascending by each referenced GateDecision.decided_at timestamp (ASSUM-008 resolution).' Verified by TestSessionOutcomeOrdering::test_gate_decision_ids_field_documents_ordering, which asserts both 'ascending' and 'decided_at' appear in the description, plus round-trip and default-empty behavioural checks.

completion_promises.criterion_text: CalibrationEvent.entity_id is deterministic from (source_file, line_range_hash) so re-ingestion is idempotent
completion_promises.evidence: CalibrationEvent.entity_id is typed as str (not UUID) and carries a description that explicitly references 'deterministic', 'source_file', and 'line_range' so callers know how to construct it. Verified by TestCalibrationEventDeterministicId: (a) entity_id annotation is str, (b) description contains the determinism contract, (c) re-ingesting two events with the same deterministic id yields identical entity_ids — the idempotency invariant.

completion_promises.criterion_text: redact_credentials() function implemented with documented regex set
completion_promises.evidence: Implemented redact_credentials(text: str) -> str in src/forge/memory/redaction.py covering all required patterns: 'Bearer [A-Za-z0-9._-]{20,}', 'ghp_[A-Za-z0-9]{36}', 'ghs_[A-Za-z0-9]{36}', 'github_pat_[A-Za-z0-9_]{82,}', and word-bounded long-hex '[0-9a-fA-F]{40,}'. Each pattern's justification is documented in the module docstring (ordered most-specific-first). Function is pure (no logging, no I/O), idempotent, raises TypeError for non-str input. Verified by tests/unit/test_redaction.py::TestPurityAndApi::test_module_docstring_documents_each_pattern which asserts every pattern's justification phrase appears in the docstring.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Unit tests for redact_credentials() covering each pattern (positive + negative cases, including overlapping matches and unicode)
completion_promises.evidence: tests/unit/test_redaction.py has 31 tests organised as: TestBearerPattern (3 cases), TestGithubClassicPattern (3), TestGithubServerPattern (3), TestGithubFineGrainedPattern (3), TestLongHexPattern (3), TestHexFuzz (3 fuzz tests of 40/40/20 random hex blobs each), TestOverlappingMatches (4 tests including 'github_pat then bearer no truncation' and 'no original credential value survives in overlapping input'), TestUnicode (3 tests covering Japanese/Arabic surroundings and emoji-adjacent hex), and TestPurityAndApi (6 tests including caplog assertion that the function does not log the original).

completion_promises.criterion_text: Unit tests confirming model validation rejects empty/missing required fields
completion_promises.evidence: TestModelValidationRejectsBadInput in tests/unit/test_models.py covers each model: missing entity_id on GateDecision, empty stage_name, score outside [0,1], empty capability on CapabilityResolution, missing gate_decision_id on OverrideEvent, missing approved on CalibrationAdjustment plus a positive companion that supersedes defaults to None, invalid outcome literal and empty build_id on SessionOutcome, empty entity_id and empty source_file on CalibrationEvent. All assert pydantic.ValidationError is raised. Models use ConfigDict(extra='forbid') and Field(..., min_length=1) on string fields that must be non-empty.

completion_promises.criterion_text: All modified files pass project-configured lint/format checks with zero errors
completion_promises.evidence: Project does not configure a Black/Ruff hook or [tool.ruff]/[tool.black] section in pyproject.toml that I can run inline. Code follows the project's documented style (PEP 8, 88-char line guideline, type hints, snake_case/PascalCase, docstrings on every public symbol) and matches the conventions in adjacent modules (src/forge/gating/models.py, src/forge/config/models.py). All 70 of my unit tests pass (PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m pytest tests/unit/) so there are no syntax/import errors. Marking 'uncertain' rather than 'complete' because no formatter ran — Coach should run the project's lint pipeline if one is configured at the harness level.
